review_text: After watching David Chang on Munchies and Buzzfeed's Worth It series, I've been hyped to try Momofuku and Milk Bar ever since. Much respect to the chef! To be honest though, I didn't enjoy the samples I had of birthday cake and crack pie soft serve. Those flavors were overbearingly sweet in my opinion. But the cereal milk with Frosted Flakes definitely brought me back to my childhood days - scarfing down the last few bites from a cereal bowl while watching cartoons. :") Aside from the soft serve, Milk Bar's cookies aren't bad either. I have yet to try the crack pie but if it's as sweet as the soft serve I'm going to have to pass on that one. But this place is worth the visit overall, especially if you're in Vegas for vacation and want to treat yourself to something sweet!

I've been to this location twice- once in March and once in July and both times I've had a 5 star experience. Location by the 16 Block inside the cosmo Milk Bar is the PERFECT treat any time of day and night! On weekends they have late hours which is a plus when working tradeshows late and having client dinners. There can be a line at times but when I've been the workers behind the counter have been pretty efficient with moving through it. Don't be sticker-shocked. These prices seem similar as they were in NYC and I happily will pay $6 for my favorite cereal milk ice cream. I usually end up buying the 6 cookie deal ($22) to take home to family or friends (they travel really well! Cookie tins are available too) The seasonal cake flavor in July was Elvis cake- Peanut butter, chocolate , peanut brittle, and banana- and my goodness was it tasty- super rich but most of the Milk Bar cakes are. I brought it to my hotel and stuck it in refrigerator to share later and it kept well which is another bonus. Christina Tosi keeps coming out with more and more flavors but you can tell she puts so much thought into the development of her treats along with the quality!

I had no idea Milk Bar had a Las Vegas location. I was so excited to come here and it didn't disappoint at all. What an amazing treat! We bought a lineup of all the popular items. You can't go wrong with the soft serve and cookies. Really, you can't go wrong here at all. If you've never experienced a treat from Milk Bar, then get ready for all your 8-year old dreams of sugar and desserts to be released. The customer service was awesome. Special shout out to Precious for making us feel so special.
Crack pie was amazing and everyone working there was very happy and engaged I definitely will be returning

This was our first visit to Milk bar and overall we had a good experience. We ordered the greatest hits so we could try all of the most popular items and we also got a special knights milkshake. I was shocked (in a good way) at how good the cereal milk soft serve was. My boyfriend adored the knights shake but the taste of the scotch was a little too strong for me. The reason we gave it 4 stars was due to the fact that the space is really small and crowded which made us feel awkward and rushed. The line even on a Sunday evening was really long. Although it did move fairly quickly.

Since this was probably our only opportunity to try the famous Milk Bar, we decided to go for the tasting. The cake pop was incredibly sweet (as I think most are), The soft serve with corn flakes was good, the cookie was just ok, and the crack pie was very good. Probably wouldn't return to spend that much unless it was for the pie.
Milk Bar is what dreams are made of. Their cakes and shakes are unbeatable and the perfect way to end a great day in Vegas!!

mmmmm Yummy in my tummy!! This was a late night snack at like 12 am so we walked down from our hotel room and walked to this place. The cereal milk soft serve is featured here and it tasted like what milk would taste like in your cereal bowl LOL! It was tasty(but a really light taste) and just something to satisfy the cravings. Yea the price is a little high so hopefully there's a price reduction?

It's really hit-or-miss at Milk Bar, but for the most part their desserts are way too sweet. I saw the chef's episode - Christina Tosi - on Chefs Table on Netflix and have been to Milk Bars across the country and I don't recommend anything really, which is a shame because I have lots of respect for the chef herself. Here's what I've had: It was our friends bday so they brought over these Milk Balls to Momofuku to end the meal. The only cute thing about it was the candle. It was just a crumbled up former-cake dusted with too much powdered sugar. I'd be embarrassed to serve this to people if I was the owner, even if it was free (it wasn't, we paid for this abomination) 1/10 Crack Pie - (now it's known as the milk bar pie) - again, too sweet, but edible. 5/10 Compost cookie kit - this is the only reason I'm bumping up my review. I brought home a tin with the ingredients from my trip to DC years ago and it was really fun to make and not bad at all! 8/10 Assorted cookies- got these for my bday this year and couldn't finish them. Way too sweet and hard/crumbly! 4/10 Overall everything is way too sweet at Milk Bar. I heard their cereal milk soft serve is good, so maybe... the 5th time I visit one of their locations... I can try it one day.
Service was excellent and I was intrigued by the Crack Pie flavor, seeing that our local Milk Bar does not serve that. Server was also helpful by offering a larger container to prevent spillage.

Had to try Milk Bar because of all the hype. We managed to go when it was crazy so there was a long line to wait for your cookies/sweets. I ordered the compost cookie because that's what I've heard you have to try. Overall it was just eh. Don't get me wrong if you need sugar then it will do the trick, but I would honestly rather eat my sugar in a different form. The cookie was a little chewy, and there was an unpleasant crunch from some of the nuts where I thought I would break a tooth. I don't know; I don't see the hype. I think next time I will go elsewhere to get my sugar fix.

What?!? 7 bucks for a non special super small milkshake? Went to Gordon burger joint got a milkshake which was way better way bigger and for the same price. Step your game up. Can't believe this place has business since it's so simple. Could get a better shake from any fast food joint. Pass this place up

Everything was amazing -- I'll be visiting every day of my trip. Today's order was cereal milk soft serve with corn flake topping, crack pie soft serve and birthday cake truffles. I'd been to the New York location five years ago and been dreaming of a return visit ever since. My memory serves me well because everything was even better than I remembered. Tomorrow I'll be back for crack pie, a compost cookie and more soft serve!

Cereal milk soft serve! So good I think I could eat a pint by myself in one sitting. It's not overly sweet with a delicious malt taste like the whoppers malted chocolate balls from my childhood! Surprisingly refreshing for a soft serve. Definitely get it with corn flake topping! Wouldn't be the same without it. Been wanting to try it in NY but glad to finally try it here!

Having lived in New York for a bit, I went to Milk Bar constantly and I am such a big fan of it...so of course I had to visit the one on the Strip. The line was long (of course) but it was so worth it. I went with the classic cereal milk soft serve with cereal flakes on the outside and it hit the spot. Brought my friends who had never experienced Milk Bar and they were astounded by all the different wacky combinations. One of my friends got the sweet potato soft serve and it tasted so so so good. Another got the cereal milk soft serve affogato, which looked incredible.

I missed the Milk Bar Yelp event and was surprised by the low ratings when this business opened, so I had to check it out myself. I have very mixed feelings about how to rate this place because some of the reviews are nitpicky as hell (but they bring up a lot of valid points), but the product is pretty good. Worth the Strip price-hike though? Debatable. LOCATION: Inside the Cosmo, easily accessible from the parking garage. Walkup counter, no dedicated seating, Chandelier Bar has couches and Eggslut has counter seating nearby. Other locations are walkup only, so this shouldn't be an issue. But a lot of people dislike it. PRICING: Compared to NYC locations, prices are a bit higher (+75c per cookie, +$1 for Crack Pie Slice, +$1.50 soft serve, +$3 for greatest hits combo, etc.). Similarly, their Milk Quakes are $12 compared to other alcoholic shake options nearby that are $15. But the other place has larger portions and great presentation, too. SERVICE: Fine. No complaints. My order was taken by friendly staff. I waited on one of my items for a few minutes and a staff member asked what I was waiting on, noticed it had been forgotten, and made it immediately. PRODUCT: Sampled a little bit of everything with the Greatest Hits Combo ($19). There are also mixes, cookbooks, tote bags, scarves, and such available for gifting. Cereal Milk Soft Serve- other reviews say the flavor is bland and portion is small, but I could barely eat half of it. I'm not a corn flake fan but the texture was appreciated and enhanced the flavor, which had a nice pinch of saltiness to it like you really get at the bottom of a bowl. Compost Cookie- #nocrunchycookies! This had the perfect texture of soft and chewy, but not falling apart. Interesting combination of flavors but I can't really say I liked it. Maybe I'll try other cookie flavors in the future, but the NYC price is a lot more appealing. Birthday Truffles- I couldn't decide if I liked this. Nice hint of bday cake flavor but really sweet. Only ate one of the pack of three and that was enough. This might be fun for gifting though. Crack Pie- It's like pecan pie without the pecans and I like that it isn't too sweet but I'm not about to get addicted. As others have said, the slice is tiny. For the price, it should be deeper/taller. It's a nice little treat but the cardboard sleeve packaging preventing you from seeing the portion size is a bit deceptive. Birthday Cake!- Okay, full disclosure, I got the cake free through an IG promo. The 6" regularly retails for $56. The portion claims to feed 8-12, and I'd say that's accurate. It's fairly tasty and not too sweet, and I like how the crumbles add texture. The "naked" decoration style also keeps the ratio of cake to frosting more even, and the multiple layers are both pretty and help to distribute the frosting. Would I eat it again? Probably. Would I pay nearly $60 for this cake? Probably not. Unclear why the crack pie is available whole or sliced but their self-proclaimed "fan favorite" cake is not. In short, it's A-OK. It's exciting that we have a Milk Bar location on the West coast and I'm glad I tried it, but even if I happen to be at the Cosmo, it's not a must-go place for me. Even if there isn't a line.

Of course whenever I go to Las Vegas (like once a year), I look up all the cool insta-worthy food places to try...LOL. This cute little place is located on the SECOND FLOOR of The Cosmopolitan Las Vegas. I had a bit of trouble locating this, but realizing the arrows on the signs I was able to find it haha. Keep in mind, this place is actually pretty small. The Milk Bar is directly right next to Momofuko which took part in developing this dessert place. I was super super anxious to taste their soft serve (that I've seen in pics on the insta from the NY location) so I could see just HOW good it really is. They serve pies, cookies, drinks (with or without coffee), and of course their soft serve ice cream. Their ice cream is actually pretty pricey starting at around $6-7. You get options for toppings such as fruity pebbles or cereal, chocolate chips, and even sprinkles. But still, pretty pricy. The amount they give you ALMOST makes up for that, but not quite all the way. My sister didn't really find the taste of the cereal soft serve appealing, but for me it was pretty dang good, but not enough to amaze me. Compared to other places I've tried, Milk Bar is definitely up in my Top 4. It's similar to MilkCow in Fremont but a more sharper/unique taste. It's simple yet so interesting and different. I'd give this place 3.8 stars, but rounded up to 4 because it was both aesthetically pleasing and appealing to my tongue.

I usually come here to just buy the cookies. But this time I decided to try some of their other desserts. Got 6 cookies (in a tin can is $12), crack pie, and the cereal milk soft serve. My favorite cookies are the blueberry and cornflake ones. Crack pie was $6.50 and it's super small. Smaller than your McDonald's apple pie. It was very rich and I felt like I was eating a stick of butter. The cereal milk soft serve is $6 just for the ice cream and adding on cornflakes is another $0.50. It tasted like plain vanilla ice cream with buttery/salty cornflakes. Pretty pricey for just a basic cup of vanilla ice cream if you ask me. The cookies make up for these other two items. Probably will just stick to getting cookies since the other things on the menu have too much butter in them.

The staff here is very friendly and patient. They will give you samples (even after you've made your purchase and you're just curious), and they will take their time giving recommendations. I saw some customers order the Chocolate Pretzel and the girl at the counter even asked them if they wanted to try it before buying it because she said a lot of people find it too salty! Even though I think the desserts here are 3 stars, I'm bumping them up for the good service! SOFT SERVE $6|$12 * Chocolate pretzel - 3 stars This is pretty savory, just enough in a sample cup, but it would have been too much in a cup of soft serve for me. * Cereal milk - 3.5 stars This by itself is plain, just like milk. With the Cornflake crunch topping ($1.25) a nice contrast is added, and it's pretty savory! FANCY SHAKES * Fruity cereal milk w/ triple sec ($12) - 3.5 stars A good thick shake, a bit strong for me with the triple sec! PIE * Crack Pie - 4 stars Tasty but I couldn't finish this! This was crazy filling and really was all butter.

I first tried Milk in NY and loved it, so I was so excited when they opened one in LA. But since I hate LA traffic, I make it a point to go to the one in Cosmo when I'm in Vegas. You can NOT go wrong with getting the birthday cake truffles - if you get one thing from here, get that. Everything else - cookies, crack pie, soft serve, cereal milk latte - are really good too. I do like the cake truffles much better than the cake slice though. This trip I did decide to mix it up and also try the cereal milk white russian, and I wasn't really a fan of that. I thought it was just me but a couple of my friends tasted it and also said it wasn't good. For $12, I would steer clear of this one.

Oh man so disappointed. I have followed this store for 3 years since seeing it in New York and not being able to stop. I got the greatest combo with ice cream, cookie, truffles and cake. It was so so so disappointing. The ice cream is sour with corn flakes on top, the cookie has a bad after taste and the cake was raw. I did not even try the truffles. Literally we paid 30.00 took three bites and ended up throwing it all away. So sad.

I had to try this place after seeing it everywhere.... TRASHED it after 2 bites. The reviews are mixed so I wanted to play it safe, I got the cereal milk soft serve with corn flakes, sour tasting soft serve and regular ass corn flakes on top... I don't understand how this place is still in business with sooo many other choices that actually taste GOOD in Las Vegas. Save your money, you WILL BE DISAPPOINTED. McDonald's soft serve is better than Milk bar.

I realized cereal drowns in milk therefor milk is a cereal killer. At Milk Bar, I love being a cereal killer. Milk and cereal, the perfect combination. Fact: you don't need to be a kid to eat cereal. At Milk Bar they serve ice cream that taste just like milk and cereal. Milky vanilla ice cream with cornflakes. It doesn't sound like it would taste so good, but somehow it's the perfect combination. I'm not big on ice cream so this was a surprise. I've had their ice cream before at their New York location and it was yummy too. Along with their ice cream they serve an item called Crack Pie. I had never tried the Crack Pie before but I decide to go all out and took some home. The Crack Pie wasn't bad but it wasn't mine blowing. It might have tasted better if I had warmed it up but it was still good. However, it just didn't quite live up to its name. I tried the Crack Pie soft serve ice cream and I wasn't a fan. I suggest sticking to what they're known for. Get the cereal and milk soft served because you're at a Milk Bar of course.

I've heard a lot about how good their cookies are from my cookie loving friends who enjoy baking, so I was excited to run across the cookie/soft serve bar while walking around the Strip. The soft serve was very tempting, but it wasn't an option, given the fact that dinner was less than 1 hour away, but the staff here was very nice and even gave me a sample taste. The soft serve was definitely tasty but a little pricey as well. I opted to buy some cookies since I could take them with me and I got a compost cookie, cornflake-choco chip-marshmallow cookie, and the blueberry & cream cookie. Note that the cookies are cheaper if you buy 3 or more ($3 each or 3 for $8)! Compost cookie was my favorite of the 3. Sweet and salty, so many different textures and flavors all in one cookie. Yum! My wife enjoyed the cornflake-chocolate chip-marshmallow one the best. We were both lukewarm on the blueberry and cream, a little too creamy and not as flavorful as the other two in our opinion.

$15 for a slice of cake. I get it...it's Vegas...overcharge because you can. Handed to me was a freezing cold slice of birthday cake...dry, crumbly, overall just not that good. Question is- where are these cakes baked and how often? If the refridgerator is necessary, why sell cakes at all in this location. I love baking from the Milk Bar cookbook and was excited by the opportunity to try the real thing. I'm left feeling like a big fat loser.... stupid enough to think the high prices indicated fresh, quality goods. Got completely duped by all the hype.
Decent-to-good (not spectacular) desserts. Very limited seating area. Quite noisy.
Was so good! Had the Crack Pie and now know why its called that...so delicious!! Will definitely try it again today!
I have been here a dozen times to share with friends while they are visiting and the majority of their reactions are amazing. The crack pie is also one of my favorite items to get.

Stopped by here after getting some food at Secret Pizza. Got the 3 for $8 packaged cookies and a crack pie soft serve. The soft serve was way too sweet for me and my boyfriend's liking. The flavor isn't anything special either, just a hint of caramel with vanilla. The packaged cookies (Chocolate Chocolate, Compost cookie, Blueberry and Cream) that I took home were pretty good though. I recommend the Blueberry and Cream out of the three. This place is quite pricy for what they have, so I won't be going out of my way to come here.

As a long time Masterchef fan, I've been dying to try out the famous Milk Bar by dessert legend, Christina Tosi. Cue a long weekend trip to Vegas, offering the perfect opportunity to cross Milk Bar off my culinary bucket list. I shared a Milk Pie (formerly known as Crack Pie, but I guess that's not PC?) and Cereal Milk soft serve (with added cornflakes) with my partner. Both were fantastic. The "Milk" Pie (brown sugar and butter on an oat crust cookie) lived up to its former name, as it was so darn delicious. It offered every flavor and texture I'm looking for in a dessert. Sweet, buttery, salty, sticky, gooey, and simply delicious. Be warned that this is a very rich, sweet dessert. If I wasn't already in dessert heaven with my Milk Pie, my Cereal Milk soft serve brought me there for sure. I loved how the soft serve really did taste like cereal milk; like the sweet milk you drink after the frosted coasting on your cereal disintegrates into your milk. However, it wasn't overly sweet and the soft serve had a nice, mellow sweet flavor. The cornflakes were essential in adding a much needed textural element to compliment the creaminess of the soft serve. I wish there was a Milk Bar by me so I could always treat myself to one of Christina Tosi's fantastic creations. Not only is Milk Bar super cute and #grammable, but also outright delicious. I suppose my body...and wallet, for that matter, are happy that it requires a plane ride for me to get a treat at Milk Bar. If not, you'd find me here on the regular!
Nice treat. They are doing something no one else is doing. Worth a visit or two. Usually a line due to popularity.

Ok..I was so exited to try products from Christina since I watch her on Master chef and she is master baker...I was totally disappointed in her. compost cookie (had no flavor) and her milk shake with crunch, they were both totally underwhelming! I was in the Vegas Milk. The next day I went to the Hershey Store and had there Kit Kat chocolate chip cookie now that's a cookie. Needless to say I won't be going back to Milk!

This place is basically just living off its hype. In my opinion it's way overpriced, but for some reason my wife loves it, so we still get something every time we're at the Cosmo. Don't get me wrong, the desserts are pretty good, but just be prepared to pay a premium. We usually get some form of the cereal milk ice cream and the pie. The cookies come packaged and in my opinion don't taste fresh, so I wouldn't recommend them. Service has been great every time we come.

Located in the Cosmopolitan Hotel, this is a cute place to grab a dessert. On my recent trip to Vegas, it happened to be a friend's birthday and we could have gotten her a whole cake here! (We didn't because we were going to be doing some walking but still, a nice celebration option.) I tried a milkshake that had their equivalent of fruity pebbles in it and enjoyed it, the shake was just the right consistency. They also provide large straws for the shakes so if you get one with chunks of cookies etc inside you can actually get to those parts to eat them. We didn't add alcohol to our desserts but it's also an option (one shake intrigued me until I saw it had gin in it and that combo got a hard pass from me ).

Since I watched Chef's table, I was super excited to try Milk Bar. We bought the all favorite package, I think it was around $23 including cereal milk soft serve, birthday truffle, compost cookie and crack pie. They all turn out to be below average taste. They are all very very very sweet. Too sweet. I'm surprised how the show Chef's table featured the world's top restaurant Osteria Francescana in their episode 1. and Milk Bar in one of the other episode for pastry series. They are clearly not at the same level. This is my personal opinion, I am sure there are fans who just love the taste. But I truly doubt how this thing made Chef's table. Since milk bar is part of momofuku group, we also tried momofuku nextdoor and it was good!

The first time I had cookies from here was when I visited Washington DC. I'm a California girl so when I heard there was a location opening closer to me I was all for it. My go-to order is an assortment 6 cookie tin and Cereal Milk with cornflakes. The cereal milk soft serve is the best and tastes exactly like the leftover milk from a bowl of cornflake cereal.

Located right next to momofuku is the Milk Bar. The cookies are something of a must try for those cookie connoisseur. They are priced at $4 a piece or 6 for $22. The cornflake chocolate chip marshmellow cookie was superb! They also have some delicious soft serve - get the swirl of cereal milk with the crack pie. It does the trick for the hot weather.

I am not much of a fan for glorified ice cream, but this place is pretty decent. Their cookies are pretty good. They have a lot of flavors and cater to different tastes. My favorites are the chocolate ones. I think they have one called triple chocolate, and for chocolate lovers out there, this is definitely one to try. The ice cream is really good too. I tried the milk cream with cornflake crunch and I was blown away. Before trying the ice cream, I thought it was just vanilla covered in the Corn Flakes cereal. I was sure wrong. The ice cream was very delicious and not too creamy. The flakes are what make the ice cream, and they give you a healthy amount. Each bite you take is filled with bunches of flakes and they even give you a good serving on the bottom of the cup, so you aren't stuck with just the toppings on top. The flakes are very savory and each bite is filled with bits of joy. I would definitely come back here again for a nice cup of ice cream.

After dining at Momofuku next door, I bought a tin of cookies to take home from here--6 for $17. I got one of each flavor: compost, confetti, chocolate chocolate, corn, cornflake chocolate chip marshmallow, and blueberry cream. A few of the cookie flavors I had tried last year in NYC. The plastic and metal tin was cute. Unfortunately, the mf TSA screener at the airport stopped, inspected, and rubbed that solution on the edges of the tin from my carry on because he thought it looked suspicious with sharp edges...SMH...OMG!!

Just as amazing as I remembered! They had gluten free birthday cake, it was delicious and the slices are huge so more than enough to share. It's located on the second floor in The Cosmopolitan, very easy to find walking in from the strip.

Not impressed. The crack pie and cornflake chocolate chip marshmallow cookie tasted fake. The fruit cereal ice cream was probably the better out of the three items we tried. Everything was outrageously overpriced for what it is.

It's a tradition of mine to stop by every time I'm in Vegas! If you don't want to try the expensive food next door, you can come here to and also grab a slice of pizza at Secret Pizza or eat at Eggslut! My go-to is the Cereal Milk Soft Serve! The mild saltiness in it was perfect, and I love the smooth texture of the soft serve! Get the crack pie too if you want something sweet!

Tried this and was a bit disappointed as everything was mass produced. The cookies were packaged in bags and were not fresh. The cereal milk soft serve was okay nothing too special. The one thing I did like was the crack pie! It's supposed to be served cold, but I recommend getting it down to room temperature and enjoying it that way. You can't really heat it up because it's a custard pie. Overall, service was good, but wouldn't really go back for their cookies. I would for their crack pie.

The cake balls and crack pie are unbelievable. I was also super excited to try the cereal milk ice cream but it was not my cup of tea or milk. It did however taste exactly like the milk after a bowl of Frosted Flakes. Staff seems pretty busy here, but they still super helpful

Cookies were on the sweeter side. Compost cookies was yummy and a good mix of sweet & savory but too heavy for my taste. I also tried the corn cookies. It's like cornflake cookies. Tasted much better than the compost cookie. I ordered a small size soft serve. Tasted like cereal milk with cornflake topping. It's yummy if you like drinking your cereal milk after eating all the cereals.

I came here on a full stomach, but had to make some room after looking at the menu. The place: Its illuminated in the casino with the Milk sign. There is some nearby seating, but it would be good as take-out when walking around in the Las Vegas heat. The desserts: Wow. I've lived in Wisconsin (America's Dairyland) and had superb ice cream and treats while there, I think the ice cream and treats at Milk Bar LV can hold their own. The shake was incredible as the taste and texture complimented each other well. The cake was great (and I don't like cake), the crack pie and cereal milk to wash it all down sealed the deal. A lot of the nice restaurants in town have great desserts, but I would forgo the dessert menu at some of them to come here.

Years, you guys. My visit to the Milk Bar has been at least 4 YEARS in the making, when I first heard about the amazing Crack Pie and Cereal Milk ice cream. I don't even remember where I heard about it but it's been in the back of my mind since then. I even made a copycat Crack Pie recipe that won a pie contest and was AMAZING. So I just HAD to try the real thing! Ugh it was disappointing. I mean the crack pie was okay but I'd rather just have like a $1 candy bar or something. The cereal milk ice cream was interesting at first bite but after that I just didn't want to eat it anymore, and that's not like me to stop eating ice cream. I couldn't finish it. My husband couldn't either. I think he was annoyed that I'd hyped this place up so much. Sorry hon. But now I know I can make a copycat crack pie that's better than the original!

Ordered a pint, the soft serve lacks flavor, the toppings taste stale. For $18, they were too cheap to give us the clean milk bar container like the one on the menu, we got the unbranded paper cup instead. Totally not worth it, not even IG worthy

I'd always wanted to visit New York just to go into Milk, I'd drool all over their instagram pics, so when I saw they had one in Las Vegas, I was jumping for joy, so I happened to be there at the time. I purchased a hot chocolate with marshmallows, quite good, compost cookie and birthday truffles were ok. The staff at this location seemed unenthusiastic, I felt like a burden to them, and weren't helpful at all. Overall, felt disappointed by my experience.

February 24, 2019 Sunday - 9:30 PM for dessert 5 Adults Atmosphere - 4 stars Food - 2 stars Service - 4 stars Background - As were walking into the Cosmopolitan we saw ladies with ice cream. They told us we had to get some on the second floor. Atmosphere - Come off the escalator and you can see that it is just a walk up window. There is not much to the place. Food Drink - I had the fancy birthday cake, fancy means boozy. I think the hype in my head to have an adult beverage milk shake was going off in my head. But the hype died when using an extra wide straw and having issues getting any shake. I had to pull my straw out multiple times to eat the birthday cake that was clogging the straw. The other issue was with the vodka, it was not mixed within the shake very well. Some sips was okay that was a good milkshake, next sip you go wow that was some high octane fuel. Service - My wife is gluten free and he warned us the drink I ordered for us would not be gluten free, because of the cake. Afterwards I thinking what kind of vodka they use. By definition vodka should be potato and gluten free but many distillers use wheat in their vodka. Future - I will pass.

Totally disappointing given the hype. Cereal milk soft serve ($6) had a creamy smooth texture but faint flavor. A small slice of Crack pie ($6.50) was cold, mushy and just plain sweet. Everything is pre-packaged (other than coffee and soft serve) which sets the perception that it is not all that fresh.
The cake didn't have much flavor. Looked pretty though. It was pricey, but most things are in Vegas.

So we've been coming back to try something sweet and I have to say that the birthday truffles are absolutely fabulous!! I totally recommend this! Last visit, we got the truffle box, which had 4 (3 truffle) bags. I choose 2 double chocolate, strawberry shortcake, and birthday flavor. The cost for the truffle box was $27. We will also grab a few cookies. The price is a little high but we figure, hey we're on vacation :)

I love Milk!! You have to have an acquired taste to their flavors! I had the crunchy cereal shake and I loved it, tasted exactly like cornflakes with a sweet touch. If you don't like corn flakes, this place is not for you, all the cereal favors revolve around corn flakes. We also tried the strawberry and corn milkshake quake. Not as good as the milkshake in my opinion, but we still liked it. It was topped with a great corn cookie!! This place can get busy, but we went twice and managed to get our orders fast thanks to the fast staff. Give Milk a try!

Went here for a 1am milkshake and it was just alright - there aren't a great many options and the cereal milk crunchy shake that I got was pretty delicious but the quantity was pretty small and I felt that there was way too much cereal left behind in the cup that I couldn't finish and had to throw away. (Maybe use a spoon the next time? ) if I want a milkshake next time I'd prolly just go to shake shack.

Get the "crunchy cereal shake". My guess is that the negative reviews are for other things. "Cake" as a flavor sounds awful anyway. So the crunchy cereal shake. It's modest-looking, unassuming. I don't know why it's so good. I can talk about individual aspects of it but the combination is bigger than the sum of its parts. It's one of those things that you can eat a lot of. Not too sweet, just the right amount of saltiness to punch up the sweetness, and just really gratifying for something that is in the realm of a dessert. I think the consistency is perfect, too - thick but easy to drink with the fat straw. And the crunchy cereal is like the genius part of it. It's got the texture and crunchiness of Honey Bunches of Oats (with almonds) that has been sitting in 2% milk (lactose free) for the duration of time it takes to eat about 40% of a bowl. Sometimes I get impatient and just want to drink the rest of the bowl because I can't spoon it into my mouth fast enough. Maybe that's why this shake speaks to me. Anyway just get the crunchy cereal shake and you probably won't go wrong. It's a bit pricey at around 10 bucks but I think it's worth it.

I was excited to try this place! I got the birthday truffles and I finished them in less than 1 minute haha. As a big fan of cookie dough, this tasted sooooo good. I considered buying the big pack after that, but I was going to the pool party at Marquee so it was not good timing. The next day, I came back and tried the cereal milk soft serve with corn flakes topping and I was hesitant to buy it since it was $6 for a super small cup but I'm so glad I did cause it was REALLY good. It was the perfect combo and even my bf liked it a lot!!

I love Christina Tosi, and I love cereal milk just as much, so I knew I just HAD to go here. For $8 (a pretty steep price!) I got a small cereal milk soft serve with their signature cereal crunch. Overall, I thought it was just a-ok. The concept is pretty cool, and I will admit it does taste just like cereal milk, it's just not as desirable as you would expect. I don't think I would ever go out of my way to eat here again, but it was decent. My fiancÃ© HATED it. After one bite he begged me for a stick of gum and kept making faces. Overall, worth a try to say you've been here, but know that's it's probably one and done. :)

10/10. I didn't know they have a milk in Vegas and I'm so happy I got to try it hear. I got the build it yourself pint and wow! Not like any icecream ive ever tasted. I'm trying to understand how you get a burst of extreme sweetness and it literally settles in one second and it's not that sweet anymore. Soooo different. Bizarre experience - I love it

Stopped by the Milk Bar located in the Cosmopolitan during a night out with friends. My friend really wanted to get the cereal milk soft serve because she had It back in New York. They do offer milkshakes, "special" milkshakes with alcohol in them, pies, cookies, and ice cream. I believe they also have some snack foods like bagels and what not. We ordered the compost cookie and the cereal milk soft serve with sprinkles. I was shocked that It actually tasted like cereal milk. It actually tasted a lot like the Kellogg's mini wheat cereal. It was okay. The compost cookie I would definitely get again. It has everything in It!! What It reminded me of was the Kona Coffee cookie from the Honolulu Cookie Company which is my favorite cookie from there. I don't know if the ice cream is worth the price, but the cookie did make up for It. I need to buy a box of them next time!

Ummmm what else is there to say other than EVERYTHING they make is amazing! We had birthday cake, egg and cheddar bombs, and the bagel bombs. The bombs make the absolute most perfect breakfast after a late night out too. So glad it is so close to where we live so we can grab them anytime.

I really wanted to love the milk bar cake but I just thought it was ok. I prefer Susie cakes celebration cake. That said, I do like their compost cookies! I wish I loaded up on the cookies rather than the cake. The compost cookie was so good I ordered a couple boxes for my Christmas gifts this year. The online order system was easy and my relatives received them on time. Also, the cookies are individually wrapped so it stays fresh unlike other cookie delivery places. So I prefer cookies from milkbar and cake from Susie cakes.

I've heard a lot about Milk Bar, which is a thought of David Chang of Momofuku fame. The interpretation is of the last milk from your favorite cereal in an ice cream format. We tried a sample, but had no room after a big dinner. I did buy an egg and cheddar with bacon breakfast muffin and a bagel bomb in a croissant like finish. The next day, the breakfast sandwiches only required a slight microwaving to get it deliciously hot. The menu was overwhelming, and I've seen lines all the way to the escalator. I have to admit I'm a David Chang fan, so just had to try this place out!

Honestly I had their cakes and crack pie. Have not been to the actual store but was given their Birthday Cake and Crack Pie. Birthday Cake could be a little bit "too sweet" for some but to me it was more rich than sweet. The Crack PIe is the business. If you freeze it it's even better. Again very rich and flavorful and a cross between pecan pie and peanut brittle (without the nuts) but it's one of those things where the whole pie is in front of you and 10 mins later you look down and nothing is there cause your fat ass ate the whole damn thing without realizing it. It's that good imo. Will def go to the store to try other items like the Cookies and Cereal Milk.

What a sweet surprise, found a Milk Bar in Vegas :D It was nice to get a taste of this NY treat in NV. They have creative desserts which are on the pricier side, but quality ingredients are used. The employees were very friendly and had a good sense of humor. Their cookies and truffles are excellent, very unique and full of flavor.

Hit or miss. I loved the Compost Soft Serve topped with crumbled cookies; however, the Cereal Milk Soft Serve topped with corn flakes totally lacked flavor. The former was rich and creamy and the latter was flat and icy. The Cornflake-Chocolate-Chip-Marshmallow Cookie was great but it is strange that they are pre-packaged. Service was confusing and not very friendly.

Milk Bar, located in the Cosmo, fittingly right next to momofuku, is a great spot to satisfy your sweet tooth! So many options - cookies, cake, crack pie, soft serve, cake pops, milkshakes. The best thing to get here is the soft serve hands down! You can split even the smaller size into 2 flavors and the best is the cereal milk and chocolate pretzel side by side. Add the corn flake crunch - they put it on the top/sides and there's a surprise layer on the bottom too! You will not be disappointed. Also delicious as an affogato and less sweet. Cookies are great to take on the go as they are packaged and you can also get a gift box to bring back as well. Unfortunately they also taste a bit like they are packaged too. Nonetheless, the best flavors are the corn cookie and compost cookie! The rest of the treats, like the crack pie or milkshakes, are good for a one time try or a taste but probably not as repeat orders since they are really super sweet.

The cashier was not super nice she seemed rushed and we were bothering her. I ordered the "crack pie" and the "blueberry and cream" cookie. The pie was super sweet! It's sorta like a pecan pie with out the pecans. It was good but nothing I would go out of my way for. The blueberry cookie was good once again was pretty sweet. I don't think I would go there just for that. I also sampled the soft serve.. the white one was horrible and the brownish one was ok.. sorry they were my friends samples so I don't what the flavors were. I don't know if I would treck the strip just to come back here but if I'm in the area I I might stop and try something new.

I was so happy to read about this being in Las Vegas as well... I always loved the one in New York City and everything is pretty much the same here, except some awesome shakes that are only in Vegas! The famous pie slice and the corn meal cookies are always a must!

Best cookies ever! I first tried them when my friend brought them back for me from NYC. When I found out there was a location in Vegas inside the Cosmopolitan hotel, I knew I had to make a detour and get more. All of the cookies are excellent, but my favorite is the compost cookie. Aside from the cookies, I've also tried the birthday cake, which tastes exactly how you'd imagine - like the nostalgic boxed funfetti cake, but better. I've also had the milk-flavored soft serve they're famous for, which isn't my favorite. It tastes exactly like milk, with minimal sweetness. I'd recommend the crack pie soft serve with cornflake topping - the saltiness of the cornflakes works well with the ultra-sweet soft serve.

I had crack pie! It was delicious. I wish it had been a freshly sliced pie, instead it was pre sliced in a cardboard container. It was teeny tiny. I was surprised at how small the slice was for the price. I probably would not go again, but I can now say I had crack pie.

First of all, I LOVE CEREAL. It was all I ate as a kid. The cereal milk soft serve is summer of 1998 on a Saturday morning watching Street Sharks and Double Dragon, distracting me from my cereal and letting the milk soak up all the sugars to become a little extra dessert at the end of the "meal". Textures of the ice cream and corn flake bits were premium. Appreciated every bite. Will be back!
You need to go here early, they run out of items very easily. Enjoyed the chocolate birthday shake.

Finally, a frozen yogurt place that doesn't feel like I ate all the calories. Their cereal milk is awesome with the mixture of the chocolate (forgot the title), it was pretty light and creamy at the same time. The lady at the counter was very helpful as it was our first time trying everything. She gave as a descent sized free taste for a couple of flavors. She also explained well, the different types of cookies they offered which we tried as well. The compost cookie was good but so was the corn! Yup corn in cookies ;) Hope the LA location opens soon!

Plastic wrapped cookies and five dollar jars of dry cereal. We tried the compost and marshmallow/whatever cookies and you really just taste sugar above the other flavorings. I am still going to try the original Milk in NYC because grabbing the cookies when they are fresh baked is probably a completely different experience.
Cereal Milk ice cream is excellent! Light and fluffy and delicious! Not too much sugar either which is perfect for me.

Milk Bar is where it's at for dessert. First off, get a slice of crack pie to go. It is expensive at $5 for a small slice, but it's so delicious that it's worth it. Another must is the cereal soft serve. Ask for a sample. It tastes just like the milk after you've eaten all the cereal! You can add cornflake crunch to it. Another good idea is the compost cookie!
Nom nom nom - we always stop here for treats. Cookies are just the right amount of chewy and the flavors are unique. Cake truffles are yummy, too. And the people working there are always nice.

I really wanted to like this place more, but it's only okay! I had tried most of the treats at the Yelp event, but really wanted to try to soft serve and some of the other treats I had previously sampled. Tip number one: get there early! If not, try to order online. They were out of some of the treats I had my heart set on. I ended up getting the crack pie milkquake, the compost cookie and a slice of crack pie. The milkquakes are priced at $10, and is maybe around 16oz.. don't quote me on the size. It seemed pretty small for the price, but I wasn't able to finish it. The crack pie/cereal milk combo is super sweet and rich. I definitely wouldn't want it again unless it came in a mini version. The crack pie was crumbled into the soft serve in large chunks. Had it been better incorporated, it may have enhanced the taste. The compost cookie is good.. I have previously reviewed it, so I won't go into great detail. The price is a bit much though.. $3 plus tax. If you want to try the soft serve, they offer generous sample size. I tried the cereal milk soft on its own, and I wasn't inpressed. It does taste a lot like cornflake milk, which is pretty neat, just it's not my favorite combo. The crack pie is priced at $6.50 plus tax.. ouch! It's good, but not worthy of its name or price tag. Overall, it's a neat place to try. The best thing they have, the cake, is way overpriced. I'm not sure if I'll return. Maybe if I'm in the area?

You have to get the cereal milk soft serve with extra cereal flakes (sweet and salty?!?! yaaassss!). You will turn over in your grave with regret if you don't try this dessert at least once! I guess my love for this soft serve comes from childhood nostalgia because I've only tasted something like this in Korea when I had cereal milk as a youngin! So freaking good! I also really enjoyed the crack pie and birthday cake!!!

I feel like Milk Bar is the backup plan for people unwilling to wait in line for an hour for eggslut. I'm so glad we are those people. Milk bar has these little bagel pops that is just a bunch of cream cheese stuffed into a bagel roll. They also have drunky milk shakes and coffee (hair of the dog) that are surprisingly strong and can get a good morning buzz going. Don't judge, it's Vegas! We went back twice because they get people through the line quickly, and their drinks and treats are tasty. The staff was really friendly too, which is something that is not always easy to find in this city.

It is worth a try if youre in the cosmopolitan but it is quite pricy for what you get! The cereal milk ice cream was pretty good and ive never really had ice cream like it before. I really liked how it wasnt too overpowering yet had a nice smooth flavor. The crunchy cereal is definitely a good addition to it. I also ordered the crack pie due to all the good reviews but thought it was way too sweet. Would definitely pass on the pie if you arent a huge sweets person.

Despite the mediocre reviews and people's disappointment over the hype of Milk Bar, I still have to praise their soft serve. People say smell and taste illicit the strongest of memories and that's probably why tasting cereal milk soft serve topped with cornflakes is so nostalgic for me. I realize it is probably one of the most overpriced soft serves in the country, but I will gladly pay $6 (+$1 cornflakes topping) for a piece of my childhood. The soft serve is thicker than typical and each spoonful literally reminds me of the bottom of a bowl of cornflakes. Outstanding. As for the other soft serves, I wasn't a big fan of the fruity [pebbles] soft serve or the crack pie version. For me, I like it pretty simple. They do offer samples for you to try should you want to taste before ordering. In terms of the cookies and pies, these definitely go into the overpriced and over hyped category. Years ago when I first tried their cookies at the original Milk Bar in NYC, the cookies were sold in display cases instead of being wrapped in cellophane. At that time, I thought the compost cookie was the best thing since sliced bread. Now, I think the cookies taste stale and mass produced. The crack pie is also no longer crack to me. It was so overtly sweet that I couldn't understand why I liked it before. However, that isn't to say I won't return for the soft serve. Yum!

We ordered the Cereal Milk Soft Serve with cornflakes and two other shakes. I fed my 2 year old son the Cereal Milk Soft Serve at 12:30 am. He woke up at 5am vomitting. He threw up three times since this morning. We are on our way back to California to bring him to Urgent Care. I also noticed this morning the guy handing out the orders really smell funky. Severe bad body odor. Should they be putting some attention to their staff's hygiene specially they are handling food. Traumatic First time experience here. Not coming back for sure.

I first had this in NYC where I thought the hype was overhyped. To my surprise, it was not. This stuff is slammin and everytime I visit NYC, DC, and LV, i gotta hit up milk bar for my milky milky fix.... except don't hang out with me after I've indulged in this because milk ain't the best for an Asian tummy.... NOTE: The prices at the LV location are higher than NYC and DC Price: About $6 for a one size soft serve cup + $1.25(?) for crunch flakes Food: Their birthday cake didn't taste as good as the one I had in dc... plus it was like $3 more expensive for the slice. Not sure if i'd make the trek to the Cosmo next time to hit this bad boy up, but we'll see. Def will hit it in NYC though.

The milk bar pie aka crack pie was highly recommended so we dropped in after having lunch at Wicked Spoon to pick up a couple of slices. The pie was dense and the taste just didn't blow me away. Sweet and buttery but not in a yummy way. Certainly not worth the 7 bucks a pop. And I told the person that recommended the pie to me just that. No hard feelings lol. Perhaps the other desserts such as the cereal milk would've been a better choice. I would stop by again to try something else when I come back to The Cosmopolitan to try Momofuku next door. Otherwise it's a pass for me.

This review is only for the cereal milk soft serve which I got with cornflake crunch. I've been wanting to try this for a long time now, ever since I first saw it on a NYC foodie page when they first opened. However, I think it has been way overhyped, at least for myself. :( I really didn't appreciate the flavor and actually thought it had a weird after taste. It was about $8 and I'm not sure I would get this again. I am open to trying the other options on their menu, such as the crack pie so I may come back! I'd recommend coming here or to a similar location to try it out for yourself!

Crack pie. It keeps you coming back for more. Very aptly named. I really have met very few pies that I disliked so of course this was a hit with my (taste) buds. Unfortunately, I had to share with my husband. FYI there does exist online a recipe for this dish although it does taste better if you a) spend 0 time slaving over making your own pie and b) spend a lot of $$$ purchasing it. Their soft serve I thought was overrated. Then again, I've never been a fan of the residual cereal milk, always tossing it down the drain. This is a little bar-ish setup in the Cosmo food court. So many new places here now! A glutton's paradise

CRACK PIE. There's a reason why it's called crack pie. It's just filled with butter and sugar, but its addicting. A little pricey, ~$6 a slice or $56 a whole pie, but totally worth trying... over and over again... LOL. I love their cookies too, it's more expensive in Las Vegas than New York, but you can't blame them. I really enjoy the blueberry & cream cookie. I like it when it's warm, so microwave it for ten seconds. I also enjoy their soft serve. They do split flavors too. I got the regular cereal milk and the crack pie soft serve served with some salty topping. It's delicious, although the salty topping gets tiring after a while. It is so hard not to spend a lot of money here. I always end up walking away with more than $75 worth of goodies. I just wish it was cheaper. Hurts my health and my wallet every time I buy cookies and crack pies from here, but worth trying at least once!

Was super excited to try Milk bar with all the hype and after watching the Netflix special. Ordered the greatest hits which is a nice mix of options (cereal milk soft serve, crack pie, bday cake truffles, compost cookie). Ate them in that order and it definitely declined in quality as we went - by a crazy amount. Cereal milk soft serve - amazing. I thought this was kind of gimmicky but it pleasantly surprised me. The taste is more subtle than I expected and I loved it. Crack pie - I was looking forward to this the most and it met my expectations. Love the chewy texture and flavour. I didn't find it too sweet and I'm not big on sweet. It's buttery and as someone mentioned has a hint of citrus (although there's no actual citrus in it) which is odd but I really enjoyed. Bday cake truffles - getting a little disappointing. Still enjoyable, nice texture but tasting pretty artificial and not fresh. Compost cookie - huge dive in quality. Honestly it was gross. Tasted so artificial, fake sweet, not fresh, yuck. Also had a coffee which was a nice surprise - found it delicious! So definitely worth a visit but unfortunate that some of the items were a miss for me. Not sure if maybe the quality/freshness has declined as this brand has grown. Would def recommend the cereal milk soft serve if you only want to try one thing. In general this place doesn't seem to have a focus on quality ingredients which thankfully is more on trend in general and could hurt milk bars longevity.

We have had the birthday cake delivered to us (highly recommend) and had to come to the Vegas storefront. We got a slice of birthday cake and a cup of the cereal milk soft serve with corn flake topping. If you love sweets like we do, you can't go wrong here. Bring your dollars though!

I was so excited to go to Milk from all of the pictures i see on social media. I got 3 things at Milk. First since I am a cake lover I got a slice of Birthday Cake which is a vanilla rainbow cake, rainbow cake crumbs, sprinkles, vanilla frosting. The cake was moist and and the frosting did have a big vanilla taste but as far as cakes go it was a little underwhelming. Really nothing special with that slice of cake. The second thing I ordered was the Crunchy Cereal Shake which was made with milk, cornflakes, brown sugar and a pinch of salt. It did taste like cereal like I expected but all I really did was pay $9 to drink cereal, nothing special there either. The last thing I ordered which was the best thing was the Crack Pie. I can see why this is there fan favorite because it is addicting as if you were doing crack. Haha. The Crack Pie has a toasted oat crust and a gooey butter filling. So you get that crunch of the crust with that creamy gooey filling. This is the one thing you must have at Milk, but overall this place was pricey and not as exciting as it has been made out to be. BrandonFitFoodie

The most unique ice cream I've ever tasted. It's ADDICTING! I always order the soft serve with the cookie crunches they add. Their milk bar is delicious, pretty much everything is amazing. It is a bit expensive but worth it!

I got the infamous cereal milk soft serve with cornflake crunch. It was good but don't see myself getting it again because of how overpriced it was. The single is $6 and $1 extra for the cornflakes (which was a bit skimpy). On a better note, they offered me a free cookie.

Stumbled upon this place for coffee and a cookie... Almost $10 later I got what I ordered. The coffee was fine nothing spectacular. However, the cookie MYLANTA - nastiest cookie I've ever had in my entire life! My best friend got one also and both of ours ended up in the trashcan. Not only Is $4 for a single cookie very expensive it's also BS when the the cookie is as dry as the desert. Absolutely awful.

I tried the normal cereal milk one. I wanted the birthday cake one but it had eggs and I am allergic. So the cashier let me have the plain one (no idea if that meant it didn't have eggs, it was a weird transaction). Anyway, as for the hype...I don't get it. Taste literally just like milk. No other flavor...kind of salty. I had the crunch flakes on it too. Kind of expensive after all that so I ate it all even though i wasn't in love. They were also offering free samples of cookies or the ice cream to some people in line but I didn't get in on that :( But, I have a friend who went here multiple times was on their trip and tried all types of stuff and loved it. So I guess if you like that salty sweet combo this is for you.

I love Christina Tosi, but I've found that most of her stuff is too sweet (even for a dessert bar). The staff here was nice enough to let my fiance and me sample the different soft serves. I found them all except the cereal milk to be way too sweet. We ordered a crunchy cereal milk shake and a chocolate shake. I liked my cereal milk shake a lot because of the corn flakes added to it. The next day we went back and replicated our order, except this time I forgot to order my shake "crunchy." The staff was nice enough to add the cornflakes in for me, which made all the difference. We also purchased three cookies (bulk discount) but were disappointed in all of them. The compost cookie had interesting coffee notes to it, but all the cookies tasted way too sweet and honestly kind of stale. We didn't have the birthday cake truffles here, but my fiance has had these shipped to me from Milk Bar in NYC and they are delicious. I would recommend that and the Crunchy Cereal Milk shake, but not much else here.

Anybody familiar with Milk Bar's origins shouldn't be surprised to see their Las Vegas location next door to Momofuku, just like in NYC. I've been a big fan of both founders for some time and couldn't wait to try both, even if it was one of their outposts. Inadequate planning kept me out of Momofuku on my recent trip to Sin City, but I was able to try Milk Bar for the first time. It was split decision. We managed to hop into a relatively short line knowing right away what we were going to try. I have been hearing about the famous Milk Bar Pie, aka Crack Pie, for ages. I even almost ordered one on Goldbelly for mail order deliver awhile back, but couldn't talk myself into the expensive FedEx fee on top of a $51 pie (btw, they don't do mail order w/ Goldbelly now, they have their own website). She wanted to try the Cereal Milk soft serve. We were also going to grab some cookies to go for later as well, but seeing how the cookies were packaged and handled we decided against them. In fact, the whole packaging and presentation was a bit unexpected. Everything was packaged like something you would get when the goods were not made on-site. I could totally picture this stuff in a high-end grocery store. We were expecting fresh made stuff. So we passed on the cookies and took a slice of the pie and the ice cream for $12.50 plus tax and tip. We found a seat nearby (they don't have their own seating), and dug in. The pie slice was very small, but considering the ingredients and the 300 calories quoted on the label, this was probably an adequate single serving. The pie was in a little box, like a McDonald's Apple Pie. Removing it I found the slice to be extremely cold, possibly over chilled, like not quite Sara Lee frozen pie cold, but headed in that direction. The taste was disappointing. My senses wanted to fire, but something was off. She took a bite and gave her unsolicited appraisal, "the butter is stale." Yeah, it had that taste. When I made it to the back edge, there was a little more of the oat cookie crust, some of it not so compressed. That tasted pretty good. I'm not sure if I got a bad piece or I just have bad taste, but I don't get it. This was a disappointment. 1/10. I took a few bites of her Cereal Milk Ice Cream and it was fantastic. It was appropriately named. The malty flavor with the creamy sweetness tasted just like the milk in the bottom of a bowl of Corn Flakes or Rice Krispies. This stuff is so simple it's brilliant. 9/10. I'll get the ice cream again if given the chance. I'm glad I didn't order a whole pie mail-order. Still not sure about the cookies.

Stopped by Milk Bar after eating at Hattie B's while at the cosmopolitan. I had heard good reviews so I definitely had to give it a try. Luckily it was Milk Bar's "birthday" so they had a deal for the milk ice cream for $1... we added on the cereal which was a good decision! The ice cream was soooo milky (go figure) and creamy and my husband said "I feel like I'm eating cereal and ice cream at the same time" haha! Which was true! I think it was good to try, but it wasn't the best ice cream I've ever had however it wasn't bad. I'd give it a try if you stumble upon it!

Avoid this vile place at all costs... and it'll cost you a lot. Cookies are bagged in cellophane, and just look at all the nasty ingredients on the label. The soft serve ice cream has no taste, I tried a twist and could not distinguish one flavour from the other. This place is a perfect example of why the Cosmopolitan should not rely so heavily on celebrity chefs. And this one should be ashamed of herself. Judging by the lineups, it's a money maker, but don't waste yours!!!
